• The four ouer planetsae alas giants madeprimarilofhydrogen an heliu.Teyhave thick asus outer yers liquid interiors. The outer planets have numerous moons, as well as planetary rings. Juie  taafifestit Great Red Spot. •Jupiter has more than 60 moons including the four largest, the Galilean moons. Europa has an ocean of liquid water under a layer of ice where life may have formed. Saturn is smaller than Jupiter but has a large system of beautiful rings. •Titan's atmosphere is similar to early Earth's and the moon could harbor primitive life. Uranus and Neptune were discovered relatively recently since they are so far away. Uranus is tilted on its side, probably because of a past collision with a large object. • Neptune is very cold and has strong winds. Its dark spots are storms in Neptune's atmosphere.
